tips.clay:
Clay Soil Advice:
• Best Crops: Rice, Wheat, Lettuce, Broccoli.
• Problem: Poor drainage — mix in coarse sand or organic compost to improve it.
• Fertilizer: Add 2-3 tonnes of FYM per acre before sowing.
• Watering: Water deeply but infrequently. Allow soil to dry between irrigations.
tips.red:
Red Soil Advice:
• Best Crops: Cotton, Pulses, Millets, Groundnut.
• Problem: Low nutrients and poor water retention. Apply NPK at recommended doses.
• Amendment: Add 3-4 tonnes FYM + 10 kg Zinc Sulphate per acre.
• Watering: Regular and frequent irrigation — use mulch to reduce evaporation.
tips.black:
Black Soil (Regur) Advice:
• Best Crops: Cotton, Sugarcane, Wheat, Soybean.
• Problem: Cracks when dry; sticky when wet. Mulch to retain moisture.
• Fertility: Naturally high in Ca, Mg, K but low in N and P. Apply Urea 50 kg/acre at sowing.
• Watering: High water retention — avoid over-irrigation, especially in rainy season.
tips.sandy:
Sandy Soil Advice:
• Best Crops: Melons, Coconut, Groundnut, Carrot.
• Problem: Low water and nutrient retention. Add farmyard manure 5 tonnes/acre annually.
• Fertilizer: Split into 3-4 applications to reduce leaching losses.
• Watering: Frequent light irrigations — consider drip irrigation to save water.
tips.loam:
Loamy Soil Advice:
• Best Crops: Wheat, Sugarcane, Cotton, Vegetables — ideal for most crops.
• Maintenance: Excellent soil. Add FYM 2 tonnes/acre every season to maintain organic matter.
• pH: Usually 6.0-7.0 — ideal range. Monitor annually with soil test.
• Watering: Standard irrigation — follow crop-specific schedules.
tips.default:
General Farming Tip:
• Always test soil pH and NPK before planting. Aim for pH 6.5-7.5 for most crops.
• Add 2-3 tonnes of FYM or vermicompost per acre each season to maintain organic matter.
• Use bio-fertilizers (PSB, Azotobacter) to reduce chemical fertilizer costs by 20-30%.
• Rotate crops each season to prevent soil-borne diseases and nutrient depletion.

qa.answer:
Rice / Paddy Farming:
• Varieties: Swarna (MTU-7029), IR-36, Pusa Basmati-1121 for premium export.
• NPK: 120:60:60 kg/ha — split urea into 3 doses (basal, tillering, panicle initiation).
• Diseases: Blast (spray Tricyclazole), Brown Plant Hopper (BPH-resistant varieties), Sheath Blight (Validamycin).
• Water: Maintain 5 cm standing water during vegetative stage. Drain 10 days before harvest.

Wheat Farming:
• Varieties: HD-2967, WH-711, DBW-17 (Punjab/Haryana).
• Sowing: 1st–15th November. Use 40-50 kg seed/acre.
• NPK: 120:60:40 kg/ha — full P and K at basal, N in 2 splits.
• Irrigation: 5-6 irrigations critical: CRI stage, tillering, jointing, flowering, grain fill, dough stage.
• Diseases: Yellow rust — spray Propiconazole at first sign.

Cotton Farming:
• Varieties: Approved Bt cotton hybrids for bollworm resistance.
• NPK: 150:60:60 kg/ha for irrigated. Apply Zinc at basal.
• Sowing: May-June (Kharif). Spacing: 60x45 cm.
• Key Pests: Bollworm (Bt spray), Whitefly (Spiromesifen), Pink Bollworm (pheromone traps).

Sugarcane Farming:
• Varieties: Co-86032, CoLk-94184, Co-0238 (high sugar content).
• Planting: October-November or February-March. Use 2-eye setts at 75 cm row spacing.
• NPK: 250:60:60 kg/ha — split N into 4 doses.
• Yield: 400-500 quintals/acre with good management.

Tomato Farming:
• Varieties: Hybrid: Arka Vikas, CO-3. Open pollinated: Pusa Ruby.
• Nursery: Raise 25-30 days before transplanting. Transplant at 4-5 leaf stage.
• NPK: 120:60:60 kg/ha + 0.5% Boron foliar spray at flowering.
• Diseases: Early Blight (Mancozeb), Late Blight (Metalaxyl+Mancozeb), Fusarium Wilt (Carbendazim soil drench).

Onion Farming:
• Varieties: Agrifound Dark Red, Pusa Red, Arka Kalyan.
• Nursery: Sow October-November; transplant at 40-45 days age, spacing 15x10 cm.
• NPK: 100:50:50 kg/ha. Avoid excess N after bulb initiation.
• Irrigation: Stop 10 days before harvest to harden bulbs.
• Diseases: Purple Blotch (Mancozeb), Thrips (Spinosad), Basal Rot (Trichoderma soil treatment).

Potato Farming:
• Varieties: Kufri Jyoti, Kufri Badshah, Kufri Chipsona (for chips).
• Planting: October-November (plains). Use 25-35 g certified seed tubers.
• NPK: 180:100:120 kg/ha — N in 2 splits (planting + earthing up).
• Diseases: Late Blight — most dangerous (spray Cymoxanil+Mancozeb preventively).
• Harvest: Vine killing 2 weeks before harvest for better skin set.

Maize Farming:
• Varieties: Hybrid: DK-9144, Pioneer-3522.
• Sowing: Kharif: June-July. Spacing: 60x20 cm, 1 seed per hill.
• NPK: 120:60:60 kg/ha — N in 3 splits.
• Irrigation: Critical at tasseling and grain fill. 3-4 irrigations in Kharif.
• Pests: Fall Army Worm — spray Emamectin Benzoate at early infestation.

Chili Farming:
• Varieties: Pusa Jwala, LCA-206, Byadagi (dry red chili), Guntur Sannam S4.
• NPK: 120:60:60 kg/ha + Calcium and Boron foliar spray during fruit set.
• Diseases: Anthracnose (Carbendazim+Mancozeb), Phytophthora Blight (Metalaxyl), Mite (Dicofol).
• Viral diseases: Control thrips and aphids to prevent Leaf Curl Virus.

Groundnut Farming:
• Varieties: ICGS-76, TAG-24, K-6, GG-20.
• NPK: 20:40:40 kg/ha + 200 kg Gypsum/acre at pegging stage for pod fill.
• Seed treatment: Thiram (3 g/kg) + Rhizobium culture before sowing.
• Diseases: Tikka leaf spot (Chlorothalonil), Stem Rot (Trichoderma soil application).

Soybean Farming:
• Varieties: JS-335, JS-9305, Ahilya-1.
• Sowing: June 20-July 10 at first good monsoon rain. Spacing 45x5 cm.
• NPK: 20:80:40 kg/ha + Rhizobium+PSB seed inoculation saves much of N requirement.
• Diseases: Yellow mosaic virus (use resistant varieties), Pod borer: Quinalphos spray.

Fertilizer Application Guide:
• Urea (46% N): Apply in 3 splits — avoid application before rain. Use Neem-coated urea to reduce volatilization by 20%.
• DAP (18:46:0): Best applied at sowing as basal dose. 50 kg/acre is standard.
• MOP (60% K2O): Apply at basal + one split for high-K crops (potato, sugarcane, banana).
• Zinc sulphate: Apply 25 kg/acre once every 3 years for zinc deficiency prevention.

Crop Disease Management:
• Fungal diseases: Spray Mancozeb (preventive) or Carbendazim (curative). Alternate fungicides to prevent resistance.
• Bacterial diseases: Use Copper Oxychloride or Streptomycin-based sprays. Remove infected plants immediately.
• Viral diseases: No chemical cure — control insect vectors (aphids, whitefly, thrips) with systemic insecticides.
• Prevention: Use certified seeds, crop rotation, deep summer ploughing.

Pest Management (IPM):
• Sucking pests (aphids, mites, whitefly): Spray Imidacloprid 200 mL/acre or use yellow sticky traps.
• Caterpillars / Bollworms: Pheromone traps + spray Emamectin Benzoate.
• Soil pests (termites, white grubs): Apply Chlorpyrifos to soil before sowing.
• Biological control: Trichogramma cards for stem borers, Bt spray for caterpillars.

Irrigation Management:
• Drip Irrigation: Saves 30-50% water vs. flood irrigation. Eligible for 90% subsidy under PMKSY.
• Critical stages: Never miss irrigation at flowering and grain fill — yield loss can be 25-40%.
• Soil moisture: Check at 15 cm depth — irrigate when soil just starts to dry.
• Water use efficiency: Irrigate in evening or early morning to reduce evaporation.

Soil Testing Guide:
• When: Before every planting season. Collect samples after harvest.
• How: Collect 10-15 random samples from 0-15 cm depth, mix, take 500 g composite sample.
• Where: Nearest KVK, State Agriculture Department lab, or IFFCO Kisan.
• PM Soil Health Card: Free at government centres every 2 years with N, P, K, pH, Zn recommendations.

Weed Management:
• Critical period: First 30-45 days after sowing is most critical.
• Chemical control: Pre-emergence: Pendimethalin (1 L/acre). Post-emergence: 2,4-D in wheat, Quizalofop-ethyl for grassy weeds in pulses.
• Mechanical: One manual weeding + inter-cultivation reduces weed pressure by 70%.
• Mulching: Black polythene mulch eliminates weeds in vegetables and saves 40% water.

Organic / Natural Farming:
• Jeevamrit: 10 kg dung + 10 L cow urine + 2 kg jaggery + 2 kg gram flour in 200 L water. Ferment 48 hours. Apply 200 L/acre monthly.
• Vermicompost: Apply 2 tonnes/acre; improves soil structure and microbial activity.
• Panchagavya: 5 cow products mixed as foliar spray to enhance immunity and yield.
• Certification: NPOP certification for organic market premium (3-year conversion period).

Soil pH Correction:
• Ideal pH: 6.5-7.5 for most crops.
• Acidic soil (pH less than 6.0): Apply Agricultural Lime 500 kg/acre — raises pH by ~0.5 units. Apply 6 weeks before sowing.
• Alkaline soil (pH more than 7.5): Apply Gypsum 200-400 kg/acre or Sulphur powder 20 kg/acre.
• Organic matter acts as pH buffer — adding compost helps stabilize pH naturally.

Nitrogen Deficiency:
• Symptoms: Uniform yellowing starting from older leaves. Stunted growth. Pale green colour.
• Quick fix: Foliar spray of 2% Urea solution (2 kg Urea in 100 L water).
• Soil application: Apply Urea 25-30 kg/acre and irrigate immediately.
• Prevention: Split N in 2-3 doses. Use Neem-coated urea for slow release.

Phosphorus Deficiency:
• Symptoms: Purplish-red discolouration on underside of leaves. Delayed maturity. Restricted roots.
• Fix: Apply DAP 50 kg/acre as basal dose. In acidic soils, use SSP instead of DAP.
• PSB: Seed treatment with PSB culture can mobilize locked-up soil P, reducing fertilizer need by 25%.
• Note: P availability is highest at pH 6.5.

Potassium Deficiency:
• Symptoms: Scorching/browning of leaf margins. Weak stems prone to lodging. Poor fruit quality.
• Fix: Apply MOP 25-50 kg/acre as basal. Foliar spray: 1% Potassium Nitrate (13:0:45).
• High-K crops: Potato, Banana, Sugarcane, Tomato need special K attention.
• Note: Sandy soils need frequent K replenishment.

Zinc Deficiency:
• Symptoms in rice: White/pale yellow streaks on young leaves, stunted growth — 'Khaira' disease.
• Fix: Apply Zinc Sulphate 25 kg/acre to soil before sowing. Foliar: 0.5% ZnSO4 + 0.25% lime solution.
• Prevention: Apply 10 kg ZnSO4/acre once every 3 years. Most Indian soils are zinc deficient.

PM-Kisan Samman Nidhi:
• Benefit: Rs.6,000/year in 3 instalments of Rs.2,000 directly to farmer bank accounts.
• Apply: Visit nearest CSC or pmkisan.gov.in with Aadhaar, land records, and bank account.
• Helpline: 155261 or 1800-115-526 (toll-free).
• Check status: pmkisan.gov.in - 'Know Your Status' with Aadhaar or mobile number.

PM Fasal Bima Yojana (PMFBY):
• Premium: Only 1.5-2% of sum insured for farmers. Government pays the balance.
• Covers: Pre-sowing losses, standing crop damage, post-harvest losses, localised disasters.
• Apply: Through bank at crop loan time, or PMFBY portal by cutoff date.
• Helpline: 14447

Soil Health Card Scheme:
• Provides: NPK, pH, organic carbon, S, Zn levels + crop-wise fertilizer recommendations. Free.
• Frequency: Once in 2 years per farm.
• How to get: Contact Block Agriculture Officer or nearest KVK with 500 g soil sample from 0-15 cm depth.
• Online: soilhealth.dac.gov.in for registration and status.

Harvest Timing:
• Wheat: Harvest at 14-16% grain moisture (golden straw).
• Rice: Harvest 30-35 days after 80% panicle emergence; grain moisture 20-24%.
• Pulses: Harvest when 80% pods turn yellow/brown — early morning to reduce pod shatter.
• Storage: Dry grains below 12% moisture before storage. Use hermetic bags to prevent weevil damage.

Grain Storage:
• Moisture: Store below 12% moisture. Dry in sun 2-3 days if needed.
• Hermetic storage: Use double-bag system (inner polythene + outer jute) or metal bins.
• Chemical: Mix Deltamethrin dust (100 g/tonne) for long-term storage (3-6 months).
• Warehouse: Register at enam.gov.in for pledging grain against bank loans.

Selling Crops at Best Price:
• MSP: Check current Minimum Support Price at agricoop.nic.in. Sell to government procurement centres to get MSP.
• eNAM: Online mandi platform at enam.gov.in — bid from multiple mandis, get best price.
• FPO: Join local FPO for collective bargaining power — typically 5-10% better price.
• Direct selling: Rythu Bazaar / Apni Mandi — bypass middlemen, get retail price.

Composting Guide:
• FYM: Compost dung + straw for 3-4 months. Apply 3-5 tonnes/acre. N:P:K = 0.5:0.25:0.5%.
• Vermicompost: Use earthworms. Layer organic waste + dung alternately. Ready in 60-90 days. N:P:K = 1.5:1.0:1.5%.
• Compost tea: Soak 5 kg compost in 100 L water 48 hours, filter, spray as foliar fertilizer.
• NADEP: Government-promoted brick structure composting — 3 tonnes compost in 3 months.

Free Farmer Help Services:
• KVK (Krishi Vigyan Kendra): Free soil testing, seed distribution, trainings. Find at kvk.icar.gov.in for your district.
• Kisan Call Centre: Dial 1800-180-1551 (toll-free) for free expert advice in your language, 6 AM - 10 PM, 365 days.
• PM-Kisan Helpline: 155261
• Training programs: 2-day to 2-week training on crop production, post-harvest, agri-business. Usually free.

Polyhouse / Protected Cultivation:
• Subsidy: 50-65% under National Horticulture Mission for polyhouse and shade net structures.
• Best crops: Capsicum, Cucumber, Tomato, Rose, Gerbera.
• Investment: Rs.9-12 lakh per 1000 sq m. Payback in 3-5 years with vegetables.
• Apply: Contact State Horticulture Department or nhm.nic.in.

Kisan Credit Card (KCC):
• Benefit: Credit up to Rs.3 lakh at 7% interest (effective 4% with government subvention for timely repayment).
• Covers: Crop production, farm asset maintenance, consumption needs.
• Apply: Any bank branch with land records, Aadhaar, and photograph.
• Renewal: Renewed annually if no default. No interest if repaid within crop season.

Drip and Sprinkler Subsidy (PMKSY):
• Subsidy: 55% for small/marginal farmers, 45% for others under PMKSY.
• Apply: Contact district Agriculture Department or state agriculture portal. Submit land documents, bank passbook, identity proof.
• Benefits: Saves 30-50% water, increases yield by 20-40%, enables fertigation.
• Maintenance: Flush drip lines weekly, clean filters monthly.

Weather-Based Farming Tips:
• Before rain: Apply basal fertilizer 24-48 hours before expected rain. Avoid pesticide application.
• After rain: Wait 24-48 hours before harvesting wet grain.
• Drought: Apply Potassium to improve tolerance. Mulch to retain soil moisture. Use drought-tolerant varieties.
• Heatwave: Irrigate in evening. Apply Kaolin spray (3-5%) to reduce heat stress on fruits.

Quality Seed Selection:
• Certified seeds: Always use seeds with ISI/NSC/State tag. Certified seeds improve yield by 15-20%.
• NSC: Buy at NSC outlets or indiaseeds.com at subsidized rates.
• Seed treatment: Treat with Thiram/Captan (3 g/kg) + Imidacloprid (1 mL/kg) before sowing.
• Test germination: Place 100 seeds on wet cloth — germination should be more than 70%.

Intercropping and Mixed Farming:
• Popular combos: Soybean+Sorghum, Groundnut+Maize, Sugarcane+Wheat/Onion, Cotton+Green gram.
• Benefits: 20-30% more income per acre, risk diversification, better pest management, N fixation by legumes.
• Rule: Pair tall + short crop, deep-rooted + shallow-rooted, and legume + cereal.
• SRI: Transplant 8-12 day rice seedlings singly at 25x25 cm — saves 80-90% seed, increases yield 30-50%.

Bio-Pesticide and Natural Pest Control:
• Neem: 1500 ppm NSKE 5% spray for sucking pests. Spray in evening as UV degrades neem.
• Trichoderma: Mix 2.5 kg/acre with 50 kg FYM, apply to soil at sowing — controls soil-borne diseases.
• Beauveria bassiana: Spray against thrips, whitefly. Apply in cool/humid weather.
• Bt (Bacillus thuringiensis): Very effective against caterpillars. Safe for bees and predators.

Agricultural Export:
• APEDA: Key body for agri exports. Register at apeda.gov.in for export assistance.
• High-value crops: Basmati rice, spices, mango, grapes, pomegranate, onion.
• Requirements: Global GAP certification, pesticide residue testing, phytosanitary certificate from NPPO.
• Subsidy: APEDA provides 25-50% subsidy on certification, packaging, and international food fair participation.

Crop Rotation Best Practices:
• Why: Breaks pest cycles, replenishes soil nutrients via legumes, reduces weeds.
• Classic rotations: Rice-Wheat, Rice-Mustard, Cotton-Wheat, Groundnut-Wheat.
• Legume benefit: Growing any legume adds 30-60 kg N/acre through nitrogen fixation.
• 3-year rotation: Year 1: Cereal, Year 2: Legume, Year 3: Cash crop. Best for long-term soil health.

Beekeeping / Apiculture:
• Best species: Apis mellifera for highest honey yield (30-40 kg/colony/year).
• Start-up: 5-10 boxes. Investment Rs.20,000-30,000 for 10 colonies.
• Subsidy: NABARD provides 85% subsidy for SC/ST and 50% for general farmers on beekeeping equipment.
• Pollination benefit: Beekeeping in orchard increases crop yield by 20-30%.

Mushroom Cultivation:
• Button mushroom: Grows in cool weather (15-20 degrees C). High value: Rs.80-120/kg.
• Oyster mushroom: Easier, grows at 20-28 degrees C, year-round in South India. Uses paddy straw.
• Spawn: Buy certified spawn from NRCM (Solan, HP) or agricultural universities.
• Training: KVK centres offer free 3-day mushroom training with hands-on practice.

Integrated Farming (Animal Husbandry):
• Dairy: HF/Jersey cross cow gives 15-20 L/day. Net income Rs.500-800/day after feed cost.
• Goat: Black Bengal, Sirohi breeds — kidding twice/year, low investment, fast returns.
• Poultry: Kadaknath (desi breed) — higher egg price in local market.
• Subsidy: NABARD provides 25% subsidy for dairy units, poultry, goat rearing under AHIDF.

Vermicomposting:
• Species: Eisenia foetida (red wiggler) is most efficient. Available from KVK at nominal cost.
• Setup: Raised cement bed 3mx1m. Layer organic waste + moistened dung alternately (15 cm each).
• Conditions: 50-60% moisture, temperature 15-30 degrees C. Cover from sunlight.
• Ready in: 45-60 days. Selling: Rs.8-15/kg. 1 tonne/month from 10 sq m unit.

Farm Machinery and Rental:
• CHC (Custom Hiring Centres): Government-subsidized centres offer tractors, harvesters at Rs.400-800/hour. Find at farmech.dac.gov.in.
• Subsidy: 40-50% subsidy on farm machinery under SMAM scheme. Higher for SC/ST and women farmers.
• Mobile App: CHC Farm Machinery app for locating rental machinery near you.
• Power tillers: Best for small farms (less than 2 acres) — multipurpose, subsidy up to 50%.

Farmer Producer Organization (FPO):
• What: Legal entity for collective input purchase, processing, and marketing by farmers.
• Government support: Equity grants up to Rs.15 lakh + Rs.18 lakh management cost for 3 years under PM-KISAN FPO scheme.
• Benefits: Bulk input purchase (10-20% cheaper), better sale price, access to credit.
• Form: Minimum 10 farmers; contact SFAC (sfacindia.com) or district agriculture officer.
